item.title: Looking up words in a dictionary with Neovim
item.description: When writing code in neovim, I frequently use ⌘K to look up documentation, function signatures, variable definitions, etc. In markdown files that doesn't make too much sense, but I wanted to use it to look up words in the dictionary instead. This didn't appear to be possible out of the …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Shifting the testing culture: Code coverage
item.description: How we collect, track, and use code coverage data to improve our testing culture
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Shifting the testing culture: Infrastructure
item.description: Infrastructure changes we made to optimize the ergonomics and developer experience of writing tests.

item.title: Shifting the testing culture: Motivation
item.description: Motivation for investing in automated testing and the benefits and drawbacks we've experienced at Lyft
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Migration strategies in large codebases
item.description: Strategies we employ in completing migrations in our mobile codebases
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Human factors in choosing technologies
item.description: Discussion on what human factors to consider when choosing to adopt a new technology
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Third-party libraries are no party at all
item.description: Details on how we evaluate whether to add new third-party libraries
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: iOS Architecture at Lyft
item.description: Discussion on the different architectural components of Lyft's iOS apps
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Re-binding self: the debugger's break(ing) point
item.description: Explanation of how nice syntax for re-binding self breaks the debugger

item.description: An overview of how we use Interface Builder to build the Lyft app

item.title: Keeping the Lyft iOS App Accessible
item.description: Explanation on some strategies in how Lyft keeps its iOS app accessible

item.title: Silencing NSLog
item.description: Details a way to clean up a noisy Xcode console
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Outlets: strong! or weak?
item.description: A quick overview of some best practices when creating IBOutlets
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: @objc class prefixes fixed in Xcode 7 beta 4
item.description: Back in December I wrote about what I thought was a bug in the Swift compiler that would expose the wrong class name for a Swift class in Objective-C. I then later found out everything worked as intended and I had just misunderstood what @objc() exactly did. Apparently it was …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: The power of UIStackView
item.description:
I was in the audience for "Implementing UI Designs in Interface Builder" at WWDC, where Apple touted UIStackView as a new, better way to lay out your views that in a lot of cases didn't require Auto Layout.

While the presentation looked good, I wasn't quite sure this solved a …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: @objc creates a wrong class name in Objective-C
item.description: A few months ago, I decided I'd get started porting SDCAlertView to Swift, but I was only a few minutes in until I ran into a problem I had no idea how to solve: I couldn't get my class names right in both Swift and Objective-C. Even though there are …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Dev-only iPhones
item.description: With today's release of iOS 8.1, most app developers are looking at 4 significantly different iOS versions they will be supporting: iOS 7.0., iOS 7.1, iOS 8.0, and iOS 8.1. Factoring in the number of devices (iPhone 4S, iPhone 5, iPhone 5s, iPhone 6, and …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: UIMenuController and the responder chain
item.description: The responder chain is a very important paradigm in the world of iOS development, and not terribly hard to understand. Dozens of articles have been written about it, and with some examples, the concept of finding a responder to a certain event by traversing a chain of potential responders is …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Xcode & Objective-C Oddities
item.description:
Any developer that has worked with Xcode to write a little more than just "Hello, World" knows that Xcode and Objective-C have their quirks. Chances are you have heard of @TextFromXcode, the Twitter handle that portrays Xcode as a high school bully in fake text conversations like this:

But it's …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets

item.title: Objective-C prefixes: a thing of the past?
item.description:
This past week, there was, again, a lot to do about Objective-C and prefixing. To most iOS developers, the story sounds familiar: one camp is strongly in favor, another camps is strongly against, and a third camp couldn't really care less.

Unless I'm unaware of any other platforms where the …
item.dc:creator: Scott Berrevoets
